summary: Comprehensive backend testing of CEX-DEX Arbitrage Bot. 18/18 pytest tests passed sequentially. All required endpoints work: root status, coin list (12 coins), live price polling (Binance+Jupiter), opportunity computation (sorted desc), stats, trades, settings GET/POST with partial updates, encrypted credential storage with proper masking, paper-mode trade execution with profit recording and DB persistence, 404 on invalid opportunity_id, 400 on live execution without keys, 400 on test-telegram without creds, and auto-exec firing trades when threshold/auto enabled. Reset settings to defaults after run.

minor.issue: Cannot clear/delete previously-set credentials. SettingsIn only updates when value is truthy (non-empty), so once binance/phantom/telegram keys are set, the API offers no way to remove them. Suggest adding explicit 'clear_<field>' flags or accepting null to delete.
Trade documents do not expose insertion order/sequence; sort uses 'ts' (ISO string) which works correctly only because ISO 8601 is lexically sortable. Consider numeric epoch for safety.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Add a way to clear stored credentials via POST /api/settings (e.g., accepting explicit null or a clear flag).

OPTIONAL: For pytest-xdist parallel runs, the settings-mutation tests can race across workers (e.g., one worker toggles paper_mode=False while another asserts True). Run with `-o addopts=` to disable parallel, or refactor tests to use isolated settings docs per worker.
critical_code_review_comments: engine.py opportunity 'id' uses time.time()*1000 in ms - identical-ms timestamps for two coins are unlikely but possible; including coin makes it collision-safe per cycle. Fine for current usage but consider uuid for absolute safety.

server.py auto_exec_task: throttles per coin to 30s but uses real time.time(), which is correct. However trades are inserted without de-duplication on opportunity_id - if two ticks generate same opp, both could fire if 30s passed.
crypto_utils.py: Fernet key loaded at import time from env. Good. mask() correctly redacts middle and exposes first/last 4 chars.
MongoDB ObjectId not leaked: /api/trades projects with {_id: 0}, /api/settings returns hand-built dict. Good.

server.py /api/execute live path: binance order uses python-binance synchronous client inside async route - blocks event loop. Should run in threadpool (asyncio.to_thread) to avoid blocking price polling. Same applies to _fetch_binance_balances.
engine.py jupiter_swap signs/sends transactions but does not await confirmation; status is set to 'filled' optimistically. Live trade outcome is not verified on-chain.
server.py: load_settings_into_state() called on every POST /api/settings - acceptable but inefficient for high-frequency updates.
No rate limiting on /api/settings or /api/execute endpoints - could be abused if exposed publicly.
test_credentials: Set test placeholder Binance key/secret during test (test_key_abc123 / test_secret_def456) - encryption verified via masked round-trip. These remain in DB after run (cannot be cleared via current API) but are non-functional and only used when paper_mode=false.
seed_data_creation: Auto-exec test inserted ~10 paper trades into db.trades during the 12s window with trigger='auto'. Stats endpoint reflects them. Not cleaned up to preserve evidence; can be cleared via db.trades.delete_many({}) if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use `python -m pytest backend/tests/test_arbitrage_api.py -v -o addopts=` to run sequentially (avoids xdist races on shared settings). All endpoints under /api prefix on BASE_URL=https://arb-profit-engine-2.preview.emergentagent.com. The opportunity IDs include timestamp suffix and change every 4s poll cycle - fetch /api/opportunities immediately before POST /api/execute. Auto-exec loop sleeps 3s. Settings doc lives at db.settings._id='bot_settings'.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found. Initial parallel test failures were caused by pytest-xdist running shared-state mutation tests across two workers (gw0/gw1) - both writing paper_mode/auto_exec at the same global settings document. Solution: run sequentially with -o addopts= or isolate settings per worker.
